tasks.name: Add KJFK METAR and TAF to Today’s Briefing with specific units
tasks.description: Find the current METAR and TAF for airport KJFK and add both to the 'Today’s Briefing' dashboard, then set the units to knots & statute miles and arrange METAR above TAF.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Weather & Briefing” option in the main navigation menu.
In the airport weather search field labeled “Airport code,” type “KJFK” and press Enter or click the search icon.
On the KJFK weather page, locate the METAR section and click the button labeled “Add to Today’s Briefing” associated with the METAR block.
On the same page, locate the TAF section and click the button labeled “Add to Today’s Briefing” associated with the TAF block.
In the top or side navigation, click the “Today’s Briefing” or “Briefing Dashboard” link to open your briefing overview.
In the briefing list, drag the KJFK METAR item so it appears above the KJFK TAF item in the ordering (use drag handle or up/down arrows).
In the briefing dashboard, click the “Units” or “Settings” button, open the units dropdown, select the option labeled “Knots & statute miles,” and click “Save” or “Apply.”

tasks.name: Plan and save a VFR cross-country from KSFO within 120 NM to the highest-rated suitable airport
tasks.description: Create a VFR route from KSFO to a destination airport within 120 NM that has a runway of at least 4,500 ft. Among these, select the airport with the highest user rating and save the route to the 'Practice Flights' list named 'KSFO cross-country #1'.
steps: From any page, click on the “Route Planner” option in the main navigation.
In the route planner’s “Departure” field, type “KSFO” and select “KSFO – San Francisco International” from the suggestions.
Click the “Find destination” or “Add destination” link or button in the route planner.
In the destination search panel, set the distance or radius filter to a maximum of “120 NM” using the slider or numeric field.
In the airport filters, set a runway length minimum to “4500 ft” (e.g., in a “Runway length” filter, enter 4500 in the minimum field).
Click the sort dropdown for the destination airport list and choose “User rating – High to Low.”
From the sorted results, click the first airport in the list to select it as the destination; if there is a tie in rating, still choose the one listed at the top.
Click the “Save route” button, and in the dialog that appears, type “KSFO cross-country #1” in the route name field.
In the save options, select the list or folder named “Practice Flights” from the dropdown (or create a new list with that name if prompted) and click “Save” or “Confirm.”

tasks.name: Add the most affordable qualifying IFR ground school course to your training plan
tasks.description: Search for IFR ground school courses, filter to options under $150 with a rating of at least 4.5 and total duration of at least 8 hours, then add the cheapest qualifying course to your training plan with an IFR rating goal and a specific target date.
steps: Click on “Training” in the main navigation and select “Courses” from the dropdown.
In the course search bar, type “IFR ground school” and submit the search.
In the filters panel, set the maximum price to “$150” using the price slider or max price field, set the rating filter to “4.5 stars or higher,” and set the duration filter to “8 hours or more.”
Use the sort dropdown on the results list and choose “Price – Low to High.”
Click on the first course card in the sorted results list (the cheapest course meeting the filters) to open the course detail page.
On the course detail page, click the “Add to Training Plan” or “Add to My Plan” button.
In the training plan dialog, select or type the goal “IFR Rating” in the goal field, then open the date picker and choose a target completion date (for example, select “December 31, 2026”).
Click the “Save to Training Plan” or equivalent confirmation button to add the course with the specified goal and target date.

tasks.name: Register for the earliest IFR Weather webinar in October 2026 with a 1-day site reminder
tasks.description: Find webinars on IFR weather happening in October 2026, select the earliest one in that month, register through the site’s form, and set a reminder via site notifications 1 day before the event.
steps: Navigate to the main menu and click on “Events & Webinars.”
In the topic or category filter, select the option labeled “IFR Weather” or check the “IFR Weather” checkbox.
Open the date filter control, set the month to “October” and the year to “2026,” and apply the date range.
Click the sort dropdown for the webinar list and choose “Date – Soonest first.”
Click on the title of the first webinar in the list (the earliest IFR Weather webinar in October 2026) to open its detail page.
On the webinar detail page, click the “Register” button (or “Sign Up” if that is the registration button).

In the registration form, fill in the required fields such as name and email (e.g., type “pilot@example.com” in the email field), then locate the reminder or notification section and select the option to receive “Site notifications only” with a reminder time of “1 day before.”
Click the “Submit Registration” or “Complete Registration” button to finish registering with the 1-day site notification reminder.

tasks.name: Save three recent Class B airspace articles with short reading time to Checkride Prep
tasks.description: Locate articles about Class B airspace published after January 1, 2022, with estimated reading time of 15 minutes or less, and save the first three matching articles to a reading list named 'Checkride Prep'.
steps: Click on “Knowledge Base” or “Articles” in the main navigation to open the article library.
In the article search bar, type “Class B airspace” and execute the search.
In the filters sidebar, set the “Published after” date to “2022-01-01” using the date picker or date field.
In the reading time or length filter, select “15 minutes or less” (or choose the option that corresponds to articles with an estimated reading time of 15 minutes or under).
Use the sort dropdown on the results list and select “Most popular” (or “Top rated”) so the most relevant articles appear first.
Click on the first article in the filtered results to open its detail page, then click the “Save to reading list” or “Add to list” button.

In the save dialog, choose the reading list named “Checkride Prep” from the dropdown; if there is an option to create a new list, type “Checkride Prep” and create it, then select it and confirm the save.

Return to the search results (using the “Back to results” link or browser back), then open the second article in the list and repeat the “Save to reading list” action to add it to the “Checkride Prep” list.
Return again to the results, open the third article, and use the same “Save to reading list” or “Add to list” button to store it in the “Checkride Prep” list.

tasks.name: Choose a preferred trainer between Cessna 172 and Piper Archer based on hourly rate
tasks.description: At a Denver-area flight school, compare a Cessna 172 and a Piper Archer that are IFR-capable and cost no more than $180/hour wet. Mark the aircraft with the lower hourly wet rate as your preferred trainer; if the rates are equal, choose the one listed higher in the comparison.
steps: Click on “Flight Schools & Aircraft” in the main navigation.
In the location search field, type “Denver, CO” and select the Denver option from the suggestions, then run the search.
In the school filters, set the minimum rating to “4 stars” so only schools rated 4.0 or higher are shown.
Click on the first flight school in the filtered list to open its detail page.

On the school page, navigate to the “Fleet” or “Aircraft” tab, then set the fleet filters to show only aircraft with “Equipment: IFR capable” and set the price filter to a maximum hourly wet rate of “$180.”
In the filtered fleet list, click on the aircraft card labeled “Cessna 172” to open its detail page and click the “Add to comparison” button or icon.
Return to the filtered fleet list (using the fleet or back link) and click on the aircraft card labeled “Piper Archer,” then click its “Add to comparison” button or icon.

Open the comparison panel or page (for example, by clicking a “Compare (2)” button), and identify which aircraft has the lower hourly wet rental rate; if both rates are the same, select the aircraft that appears first in the comparison table.
Click the “Mark as Preferred” or similar button for the chosen aircraft and save it as your preferred trainer.

tasks.name: Save the nearest qualifying AME near ZIP 30303 with early weekday availability
tasks.description: Find an Aviation Medical Examiner within 30 miles of ZIP code 30303 who performs Class 1 medicals and has weekday appointments available before 6:00 PM. Save the examiner with the earliest qualifying availability to a 'Medical Contacts' group.
steps: From the main navigation, click on “Resources” and select “Medical Examiners” from the dropdown.
In the location or ZIP field, type “30303” and start the search for examiners near that ZIP code.
In the distance or radius filter, set the radius to “30 miles” using the slider or numeric input.
In the certification or services filter, check the box for “Class 1 medicals” to show only examiners offering Class 1 exams.
In the availability filters, select “Weekdays” and choose the time range option that includes appointments “Before 6:00 PM.”
Use the sort control to sort the examiner list by “Next available appointment – Soonest first.”
Click on the name of the first examiner in the sorted list to open the examiner’s detail page.
On the examiner detail page, click the “Save to Contacts” or “Add to Contacts” button.

In the contacts dialog, choose the group named “Medical Contacts” from the dropdown (or create it with that name if necessary), optionally type a note such as “Earliest weekday before 6 pm,” and click “Save” or “Add.”

tasks.name: Post a short-field landing question in the Landings forum with specific tags
tasks.description: Create a new question in the pilot forum’s 'Landings' category titled 'Short-field landing tips for Cessna 172', with a body of at least two sentences, and add exactly three tags: 'short-field', 'landing', and 'C172'. Enable site notifications only for replies.
steps: Click on “Community” in the main navigation and select “Pilot Forum” from the dropdown.
On the forum page, click the “New Post” or “Ask a Question” button.
In the category dropdown, select the category labeled “Landings.”
In the title field, type the question title: “Short-field landing tips for Cessna 172”.
In the main text area, type at least two complete sentences describing your situation (for example, mention practicing short-field landings on a 2,000 ft runway with trees at the approach end).
In the tags input field, add exactly three tags by typing and confirming each: “short-field”, “landing”, and “C172”.
In the notification options section, deselect any email notification checkbox and select the option labeled “Site notifications only” for replies or updates.
Click the “Post Question” or “Submit” button to publish your question to the Landings category with the specified tags and notification settings.
